text: The surgeon employs a technique to separate the outer layer of the cystic structure from its inner contents without cutting into the bag itself, minimizing the risk of rupture. The outer white tissue is incised to facilitate peeling off the cystic structure, which appears sticky in the upper part.

The surgeon smoothly cuts the remaining attached part of the transparent coating, preserving the white parts. The surgical field is washed and cleaned, and a single cut is made on the white paper for even shaping before removing the plastic bag.
